 {"id":"ft-2oph2.2","title":"[ft-2oph2][perf] Reuse chunked scan buffer for definitive trigger scan","description":"# Goal
Eliminate duplicate full-buffer accumulation in the chunked scan pipeline when both trigger scanning and compression are enabled.

## Scope
- File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs`
- Reuse the existing uncompressed chunk buffer as the definitive flush-time trigger input whenever compression is enabled, instead of also appending the same bytes into `trigger_data_buffer`.
- Preserve exact batch/chunk trigger parity and compression behavior.
- Add focused unit coverage proving the no-duplicate-buffer path and unchanged trigger results.

## Out of Scope
- No changes to SIMD algorithms, trigger patterns, compression codec behavior, or runtime wiring.
- No changes to unrelated runtime/event/storage files.

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo bench -p frankenterm-core --bench scan_pipeline --no-run`

## Rationale
`ChunkedPipelineState` currently keeps both `uncompressed_buffer` and `trigger_data_buffer` populated with the same raw bytes in the common configuration where compression and triggers are both on. This inflates memory traffic and buffer growth on the hot path without changing behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealLotus","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:02:43.603376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917717Z","close_reason":"Implemented chunked scan buffer reuse, strengthened compression-enabled trigger parity tests, and validated via rch-backed scan_pipeline/proptest/bench coverage; remaining repo-wide failures were unrelated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2xnop","title":"[MEDIUM] pty CommandBuilder serde proptests skip serialized unix umask state","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit c664dc39 (test: extend pty serde proptests) added frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs, but the new roundtrip tests never cover the unix-only `CommandBuilder::umask` field even though it is part of the derived serde surface.

Evidence:
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:202-210 derives Serialize/Deserialize for CommandBuilder and includes `#[cfg(unix)] pub(crate) umask: Option<libc::mode_t>`.
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:423-425 exposes `CommandBuilder::umask(...)` to mutate that serialized field.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:35-52 `build_command(...)` never sets umask.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:57-123 only asserts argv/cwd/env/controlling_tty, so a serde regression that drops or corrupts `umask` would still pass.

Why this matters:
- This commit specifically expanded serde coverage for CommandBuilder, but on Unix it still leaves a serialized execution-affecting field untested.
- `umask` changes file creation permissions for spawned programs, so a silent serde drift here is behaviorally significant.

Requested fix:
- Extend the proptest generator to cover `Option<libc::mode_t>` on unix builds and assert roundtrip preservation.
- If cross-platform friction is a concern, add a targeted unix-only unit/proptest rather than leaving the field uncovered.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:52:51.639844Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.030677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.029943Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-326s9","title":"Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test — config shape contradicts production logic","description":"# Pre-existing mTLS test failure

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 359 verification work. The test
`distributed::tests::bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:3805` fails reproducibly with:

    client bundle: MissingClientCaPath

## Reproduction
```
cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed \
    --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls -- --nocapture
```

## Root cause
The test constructs a `client_cfg` with `auth_mode =
DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but without `client_ca_path` populated:

```rust
let mut client_cfg = DistributedConfig::default();
client_cfg.enabled = true;
client_cfg.auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls;
client_cfg.tls.enabled = true;
client_cfg.tls.cert_path = Some(client_cert.path().display().to_string());
client_cfg.tls.key_path = Some(client_key.path().display().to_string());
// client_ca_path intentionally left as None.
```

Then it calls `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, Some(ca_cert.path()))`. Inside
`build_tls_bundle`, `build_server_config` runs the mTLS branch and requires
`tls.client_ca_path` (the cert used to verify INCOMING client certs). It is
None → `DistributedTlsError::MissingClientCaPath`.

The `server_ca_path` parameter passed to `build_tls_bundle` is used by
`build_client_config` to configure the outgoing connection's trust anchor —
it does NOT populate `tls.client_ca_path`.

## The tension
- `ft-xbnl0.2.4` tick 337 test `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`
  explicitly pins "Mtls auth without client_ca_path → `MissingClientCaPath`"
  as the intended production contract.
- The failing test expects the opposite (that this config shape succeeds).

## Options
1. Fix the test: set `client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path = Some(ca_cert.path().display().to_string())` alongside the existing cert/key.
2. Restructure `build_tls_bundle` to take separate server and client CA paths, or to accept `server_ca_path` as sufficient for mTLS when the client-side bundle is being built.
3. Deprecate the failing test if the config shape was only ever tentative.

## Attribution
- Authored: jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit 147b22176).
- Never fixed or touched since.
- Observed failing on 2026-04-18 during ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification sweep.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The bead-scoped check script (`scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh`) uses `--lib tls_`
filter which does not catch this test (the name has `mtls` with no `tls_`
substring). All 73 tests in that filter pass. The failure surfaces only on
broader `--lib distributed::tests::` filter.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.2`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:36.191788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047196Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 362 (commit c799aad4): populated client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path in the test, matching the tick-337 positive-guard contract. Broad distributed::tests:: filter now 154/154 ok (was 153/154).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mtls","pre-existing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-326s9","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.143481Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-43bbl","title":"[HIGH] frankenterm-gui no longer compiles against current wgpu PollType::Wait API","description":"cargo check with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target fails in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:169-173. The code calls wgpu::PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) }, but the current enum variant has no such fields, producing E0559 for both submission_index and timeout. Result: the frankenterm-gui binary fails to compile. Fix by updating the readback poll path to the current wgpu API shape and add a targeted compile/test lane covering the webgpu readback wait helper.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:37.317757Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526765Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526442Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","title":"[ft-e34d9.10.5.2][support] Strengthen cancellation-under-contention invariants in mux migration completion harness","description":"Bounded test-only support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Scope is limited to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Strengthen the currently shallow cancellation coverage by adding a contention-focused invariant: a cancelled acquire while the only pool permit is held must fail cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious downstream mux activity, and the subsequent healthy release/retry path must restore a balanced pool. Validate with rch-only targeted cargo evidence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyRaven","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441229Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cancellation","integration-tests","test-fix","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2605,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Started bounded support slice on crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Plan: strengthen cancellation-under-contention coverage so a cancelled nested acquire while the only pool permit is held fails cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious mux activity, then prove the subsequent healthy release/retry path restores balance. File reservation is active via Agent Mail #6191; validation will be rch-only targeted cargo.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:57Z"},{"id":2607,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Patch landed in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Added a new cancellation-under-contention regression that mirrors the existing timeout-cascade recovery shape but uses user cancellation and stronger invariants: blocked acquire must surface cancellation, preserve zero available permits while the primary checkout is held, avoid incrementing total_acquired, and avoid issuing extra mock mux requests before a healthy release/retry restores balance. Local rustfmt and git diff --check are green. Targeted remote validation is in flight via rch on worker vmi1153651: env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:17:11Z"},{"id":2611,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Remote validation completed cleanly on worker vmi1153651. Command: env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1. Result: 1 passed / 0 failed. This confirms the new regression in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs correctly proves a cancelled nested acquire under contention does not leak permits, does not count as a successful checkout, does not issue an extra mux request, and still allows the following healthy release/retry path to restore a balanced pool.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:30:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-e8brh","title":"[MEDIUM] cancel_races_child_creation test has weak race coverage; passes trivially via propagation","description":"In commit 6a16ae0a (fix(ft-2xnop): add unix umask coverage ... +
cancellation concurrency tests), the test
\`cancel_races_child_creation_all_children_see_cancellation\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/src/cancellation.rs:2635-2676 claims to verify
the cancel+child race but does not reliably exercise the race window.

Structure:
\`\`\`rust
for _ in 0..20 {
    let parent = CancellationToken::new(...);
    let n_creators = 8;
    let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(n_creators + 1));

    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..n_creators).map(|i| {
        let parent = parent.clone();
        let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
        thread::spawn(move || {
            barrier.wait();
            parent.child(ScopeId(format!(\"c_{i}\")))
        })
    }).collect();

    barrier.wait();
    parent.cancel(ShutdownReason::UserRequested);
    // ... assert every child is cancelled
}
\`\`\`

Problems:

1. The barrier synchronizes the START of each thread's work but not
   the duration of \`parent.child(...)\`. On a machine with >=8 cores,
   the 8 creator threads typically finish \`parent.child(...)\` BEFORE
   the main thread reaches \`parent.cancel(...)\`. In that ordering, all
   children are pre-existing when cancel propagates — a trivial case
   covered by other tests in the file.

2. 20 sequential rounds with no scheduler perturbation (no \`yield_now\`,
   no sleep, no thread count above core count) rarely hit the interesting
   interleaving where cancel runs mid-child-creation.

3. The assertion passes even if \`parent.child()\` has a bug where it
   reads the \"parent cancelled\" flag AFTER registration but BEFORE
   propagation reaches it — because the test never creates that window.

Impact: false confidence in race-safe \`CancellationToken::child()\`
under concurrent cancel. A latent bug where a newly-created child
escapes both propagation (not-yet-registered) AND post-cancel detection
(child() reads stale flag) would not be caught.

Fix direction:
- Spawn more creators than CPU cores (e.g., 64) so some are preempted
  mid-call.
- Insert \`thread::yield_now()\` or a short sleep inside the creator
  body before calling \`parent.child()\` to widen the race window.
- Alternatively, use an inner barrier that creators wait on AFTER
  they've decided to create the child but BEFORE registration, so the
  main thread can force the interleaving.
- Consider using loom or shuttle for deterministic interleaving
  coverage instead of probabilistic races.

Severity: medium — test quality issue, not a proven correctness bug
in cancellation.rs. Filing because the test claims coverage it does
not deliver.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:25:11.821489Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:48:54.626237Z","close_reason":"Hardened in 7ac677a6: creator count 8→64 (forces preemption on any host), per-thread yield_now + modulo 50µs sleep widens race, rounds 20→64, each creator samples is_cancelled() pre-child() and the round-level outcomes are checked so the test asserts the cancel-first ordering was actually exercised at least once — if it isn't, the test fails with a diagnostic pointing at loom/shuttle for deterministic coverage. Build clean; local run blocked by unrelated macOS dyld bottleneck under load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","concurrency","review","test-gap","testing"]}

Verification: rch cargo test blocked on remote CC=/opt/homebrew/... path missing on Linux worker; local cargo test reached unrelated existing compile errors in vendored/mux_client.rs and web_framework.rs after this slice compiled.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-fbee5","title":"pattern pack discovery followed symlinks out of tree","description":"# [HIGH] pattern-pack loader: symlink-escape via discover_packs_from_dir

**Subsystem:** patterns / scan_pipeline
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs

## The hole

`load_pack_from_file(path, root)` routes through `sandbox_resolve` only when a
`Some(root)` is supplied. `discover_packs_from_dir`, used for
`.ft/patterns/**` discovery from a workspace root, was calling
`load_pack_from_file(path, None)` for every file + rules.toml it iterated —
so the sandbox check was **skipped** even though the dir itself was
workspace-attacker-controlled.

A hostile repo could ship:

    .ft/patterns/config.yaml -> /Users/victim/.ssh/id_rsa

`path.is_file()` follows the symlink (returns true), `load_pack_from_file`
opens the target, feeds its contents to `serde_yaml::from_str`, and on parse
failure emits:

    tracing::warn!("Skipping invalid user pack {}: {e}", path.display());

Which can echo excerpts of the target file via serde's error reporting
(`mapping expected, found: …`).

## Pre-conditions

* victim clones a hostile repo that contains a symlink under
  `.ft/patterns/`;
* frankenterm loads user packs at session start.

## Fix

`discover_packs_from_dir` now passes `Some(dir)` as the sandbox root to
`load_pack_from_file`. `sandbox_resolve` canonicalizes and rejects any
resolved path outside `dir`. Added regression test
`discover_packs_rejects_symlink_escape`.

## Residual risk

A top-level symlink replacing `.ft/patterns` itself would still escape
because the caller (`load_packs_from_config`) builds `ws_dir =
root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns")` without canonicalizing. That's a
follow-up — tracking separately.

## Sibling work

- 33e84436 security(patterns): add sandbox_resolve helper (helper introduction)
- e8397747 / 8e57c8e0 fix(limits): MAX_PACK_BYTES 16 MiB cap (orthogonal DoS layer)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:10.019601Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.244142Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.243799Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 6ab52765 — canonicalize + in-tree check applied to pack discovery","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ft-05hfm","patterns","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-fvt0b","title":"codec oversized-container regression overconstrains error wording","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:43:12.478186Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:17.315201Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:17.314833Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 4d6c4abc; verified with codec --lib and conformance_pdu_wire","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-trby3","title":"[reality-gap] RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite is enum-reachable but runtime-unreachable","description":"# Claim (README)

README.md:62 lists "**Replay & Forensics**: Capture, replay, and
diff decision graphs for post-incident analysis and regression
testing" as a core feature. No distinction between backends is
offered — the user's mental model is "replay just works."

# Reality (code)

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs:560-578`:

```rust
match config.backend {
    RecorderBackendKind::AppendLog => { ...opens storage... }
    RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite => Err(
        RecorderStorageError::BackendUnavailable {
            backend: RecorderBackendKind::FrankenSqlite,
            message: "frankensqlite backend not yet implemented".to_string(),
        }
    ),
}
```

The enum variant is public, serde-deserializable (confirmed by
presence on `RecorderStorageConfig.backend` field), and reachable
from any operator who writes `backend = "frankensqlite"` (or
whatever serde rename applies) in their ft.toml's recorder section.

# Failure shape

Operator configures frankensqlite → app bootstraps → recorder
bootstrap returns `BackendUnavailable` → app either:
  (a) aborts with the "not yet implemented" message (informative);
  (b) silently falls back to AppendLog (if caller does);
  (c) propagates the error and the whole watch daemon fails to start.

Which branch fires depends on callers of `bootstrap_recorder_storage`
— worth verifying in a follow-up. Best case (a) wastes operator's
time; worst case (c) is a surprise crash.

# Recommended disposition

Either:
  (a) Remove `FrankenSqlite` from the serde-exposed enum until the
      backend is actually implemented. Zero blast radius — the
      variant is only reachable by operator opt-in today.
  (b) Keep the variant but add a deprecation/preview flag that
      operators must explicitly set (e.g., `experimental_backend =
      true`) so accidental selection is impossible.
  (c) Implement the FrankenSqlite backend.

(a) or (b) ship in minutes; (c) is a subsystem-sized undertaking.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:44:30.571102Z","created_by":"reality-check","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:51:20.515092Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:51:20.514877Z","close_reason":"README + flight-recorder docs now disclose that append_log is the only shipped recorder backend and frankensqlite remains rollout/test-only until bootstrap support lands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reality-gap","recorder","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-twnmf","title":"[MEDIUM] br-43bbl WebGPU readback polling rewrite is untested at the changed timeout/callback boundary","description":"Commit 6f41130c rewrote WebGpuTexture::read() from a single wgpu::PollType::Wait call to a manual Poll loop with 10ms recv_timeout slices (crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:163-193). That changes the control flow around map_async completion, timeout expiry, and callback disconnect handling, but the test module in the same file still only covers helper routines like padded_readback_bytes_per_row and copy_padded_readback_to_image (crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:738-1003) and never exercises read() itself. This means a regression in the new bounded-poll behavior can land without any automated signal. Add a focused regression harness or extracted helper test that proves the success, timeout, and disconnected-callback cases for the new loop.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:54:02.292808Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:05.990393Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:05.990145Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-up6u2","title":"[security] mission + tx contract loaders lack size cap — OOM vector for hostile repo .ft/mission/*.json","description":"# Bug

`mcp_load_mission_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:257) and
`mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:141) both
use bare `std::fs::read_to_string(path)` with no stat-before-read
size guard.

Pattern-pack loader has this exact guard (ft-05hfm, MAX_PACK_BYTES =
16 MiB). Mission files under `.ft/mission/*.json` have the same
trust property as patterns under `.ft/patterns/*.{yaml,json,toml}` —
a hostile repo can ship them, and they're loaded at MCP tool
invocation time.

# Attack

1. Hostile repo ships `.ft/mission/active.json` = 5 GiB of garbage.
2. Operator (or agent via wa.mission_state MCP) opens the project.
3. `wa.mission_state` → `mcp_load_mission_from_path` →
   `read_to_string` inhales 5 GiB into memory → watcher OOMs.

Same attack for `.ft/mission/tx-active.json` via wa.tx_show / wa.tx_run.

`resolve_workspace_scoped_path` (mcp_missions.rs:61) blocks
path-traversal but does NOT cap file size inside the workspace.

# Fix

Add `MAX_MISSION_BYTES` + `MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES` constants (16 MiB
each, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES) with a stat-before-read check + a
second-line check after read, mirroring the pattern-pack loader
structure.

Both constants at 16 MiB because:
- mission JSON + 100 assignments ≈ 50-100 KiB realistically
- tx contract + plan ≈ similar
- 16 MiB = 200x headroom

# Tests

Regression tests similar to `load_pack_from_file_rejects_oversize_yaml`:
  - `mcp_load_mission_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`
  - `mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:25:19.149935Z","created_by":"pane3-mission-cap","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:25:19.149935Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","mission","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-uqlrq","title":"[mock] search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36 embed() is a stub that always errors","description":"search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36-38 has `fn embed(&self, _text: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, EmbedError> { // Stub: real implementation loads ONNX model; Err(EmbedError::ModelNotFound(...)) }`. The entire Embedder trait impl is a stub that always returns ModelNotFound. Zero production callers exist — tests in search/mod.rs and daemon/worker.rs explicitly verify model2vec is "retired" and rejected. Production behavior should be: either (a) implement real ONNX model loading if model2vec is still planned, or (b) delete the file entirely since it is dead code with no callers and tests actively reject it.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:43.678300Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389463Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389208Z","close_reason":"Shipped in af925d26. Audit confirmed the file was orphaned:
- not declared in search/mod.rs (no 'mod model2vec_embedder;' anywhere)
- zero constructors of Model2VecEmbedder outside the file itself
- three tests across the tree explicitly verify model2vec is RETIRED (never advertised as a tier, rejected as a selector, rejected by the embed worker): search::tests::advertised_embedder_tiers_never_reports_retired_model2vec_backend, search::daemon::worker::tests::build_embedder_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector, tests/semantic_backend_selection_integration.rs::embed_worker_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector

Decision rationale: the bead's two options (a/b) are 'wire up real ONNX impl' or 'surface via feature flag + clear error'. Both miss for orphan code with retirement tests in place:
- (a) would contradict those tests and re-expand a surface we deliberately shrank
- (b) only helps if there's a live caller who needs a clear error; there isn't one

The correct 'not supported in this build' signal for orphan code is absence. Delete the 40-line skeleton; a future model2vec revival will write fresh against whatever the Embedder trait looks like then. Also updated the stale 'e.g. Model2Vec distilled' comment on ManagedEmbedderStack.fast in orchestrator.rs to describe the Fast-tier slot generically + cross-link the retirement test.

The three retirement tests stay intact — they continue pinning the invariant.

Verification (rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target):
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features: clean
- --features semantic-search build blocked by 13 pre-existing unrelated errors in web/sse.rs (select! macro fallout from the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip); my patch only removes a file + edits a doc comment so it cannot introduce compile errors

Note: the commit that carries the change is misleadingly titled 'docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim' — another agent's linter bundled my uncommitted model2vec deletion + orchestrator comment into its own commit. The 44 lines of change are correct (40 deletions in model2vec_embedder.rs + 4 lines in orchestrator.rs doc comment); only the commit title under-describes the scope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-v0339","title":"[MEDIUM] integration_mux_checkpoint_topology post-rollback check only compares pane-id set, not tree shape","description":"Commit 30172171 fixed ft-olq2e by routing the post-rollback check through server.registry().snapshot() instead of the vacuous re-deserialization of initial_topo_json. However the new primary assertion at integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs still only compares PANE IDs:

  post_rollback_pane_ids_sorted == baseline_pane_ids

TopologySnapshot (crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs) is a tree of windows → tabs → panes, with per-window position/size/title and per-tab pane layout metadata. The registry snapshot, filtered to LifecycleEntityKind::Pane, flattens all of that to a bag of pane u64 ids.

Consequence: a RemoteRequest::Rollback that restores the correct set of pane entities but (a) reparents them under the wrong tab, (b) drops a window or tab node, (c) corrupts the split tree under a tab, or (d) restores panes in the wrong order will still pass the assertion. Rollback tree-shape correctness is therefore still not covered by this test — the bead it closes was motivated precisely by false confidence in rollback behavior.

Expected fix:
- Reconstruct a post-rollback TopologySnapshot from the live server (registry + whatever stores tab/window edges) and compare it field-by-field against the pre-expansion baseline — at least window ids + tab ids + pane ids grouped by tab.
- Keep the pane-id-set assertion as a weaker redundant guard.

Regression risk: medium. The test name promises topology validation; reviewers relying on this bead's closure will assume tree-structure regressions are caught when in fact only pane existence is.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:21.495704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017561Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017321Z","close_reason":"Strengthened rollback validation to require exact TopologySnapshot restoration, not just pane-id/fingerprint agreement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mux","review","session-persistence","test-gap"]}
-{"id":"ft-v5do5","title":"scan_pipeline: per-category trigger count parity not pinned (only totals compared)","description":"# Gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_scan_pipeline.rs` has three chunked-vs-batch trigger parity properties:
 - chunked_batch_trigger_parity (line 206)
 - chunked_batch_trigger_parity_arbitrary_boundaries (line 229)
 - chunked_batch_trigger_parity_arbitrary_boundaries_with_compression (line 255)

All three assert only `batch_total == chunked_total` via `total_matches`. None compare the per-category distribution (`TriggerScanResult.counts`).

A future refactor that miscounts one category and compensates in another — e.g., an off-by-one in overlap skip that drops an Error match while spuriously counting a Warning — would pass all three existing tests while quietly corrupting per-category analytics (rate-limit detection, error clustering, workflow routing).

# Fix

Add `chunked_batch_per_category_trigger_parity` proptest: assert `batch_output.triggers.counts == chunked_output.triggers.counts` (HashMap equality) across arbitrary chunk boundaries. Keeps the definitive-rescan invariant honest at the category level, not just the aggregate level.

# Severity

MEDIUM — no live defect, just coverage thinness. Shipping as a feature/test strengthening rather than a bug fix because the current code path IS correct (flush re-scans with the same scan_counts used by batch, so per-category counts MUST match); the test just doesn't pin it.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:02.169355Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:15.967065Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-w4znq","title":"error renderer maps typed pane-not-found to internal error","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:43:19.139058Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:55:49.736016Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:55:49.735721Z","close_reason":"mapping landed in bb844a72 while this audit was in flight; no remaining code diff under this bead","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wajba","title":"tmux cmd_queue drop-head-in-flight corrupts Guarded response routing","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs:73-91 (push_command_capped) vs :165-186 (Guarded response handler).

push_command_capped(queue, cmd) does queue.drain(..excess) when queue exceeds CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000. The drain is from the FRONT. But when TmuxDomainState.state == WaitingForResponse, the front of cmd_queue is the in-flight command — send_next_command only peeks front() and writes, it does not pop; the pop happens at the Guarded response handler (tmux.rs:172) via cmd_queue.pop_front().

Race / correctness gap: if push_command_capped runs while state == WaitingForResponse and queue.len() reaches 50_001, drain drops the in-flight head. When the Guarded response arrives, pop_front() pops a DIFFERENT pending command and calls cmd.process_result(domain_id, &response) on the wrong command — cross-wiring response payload onto the wrong command parser. Both threads hold the cmd_queue lock so the drain+pop ordering is deterministic under the lock, but the correctness violation stands.

Fix options (not applied here, pattern-level):
 1. When state == WaitingForResponse, skip index 0 in drain: queue.drain(1..=excess) (but preserves head).
 2. Drain from the back (newest-first) which discards most recently enqueued layout/session events that often re-fire.
 3. Abort the in-flight state machine (set state=Idle) and drain — heavy-handed but restores invariant.

Only triggers in pathological event storms (remote tmux lag + 50_000 queued commands) so severity is MEDIUM. The original bug fixed in 1055cffc (push-site cap enforcement) traded unbounded memory for this subtle correctness edge. A regression test would drive tmux into WaitingForResponse, push >50_001 Layout/SessionChanged events, then fire a Guarded response and assert process_result is NOT called against a different command type than the one that was sent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:32.972876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.760204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.759836Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9cc7e6c2. push_command_capped migrated from free function to TmuxDomainState method so it can consult self.state before choosing drain range:
- state == WaitingForResponse → drain(1..=excess) (preserves index 0, the in-flight cmd awaiting its Guarded response)
- any other state → drain(..excess) (retains existing drop-oldest semantics)

Both drain branches log distinctly so event-storm incidents are traceable. Lock ordering matches Guarded-handler's (cmd_queue first, state transiently inside method): no deadlock. send_next_command's cap-drain is untouched — already guarded by early 'state != Idle → return'.

Regression coverage: 2 new tests under mux::tmux::tests that each seat a distinguishable SplitPane at index 0 and push CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH more commands; one flips state to WaitingForResponse and asserts the head's get_command() text is unchanged (pre-fix fails), the other keeps state Idle and asserts the head IS drained (pins against future over-correction). Existing cmd_queue_hard_cap_drains_oldest_when_exceeded still green after the refactor.

Verification via rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target:
- cargo check -p mux --lib: clean
- cargo test -p mux --lib cmd_queue: 3/3 green.

Unrelated pre-existing proptest_pane_serde compile failure (17-tuple exceeds proptest::Strategy impl) blocks --tests build for the whole crate but does not touch the patched code path; flagged for a separate bead if not already filed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
